article_id: Article 1
text_snippet: The Republic of Armenia is a sovereign, democratic, social, and rule-of-law state.

article_id: Article 10. Guaranteeing Property
text_snippet: 1. All forms of property shall be recognized and equally protected in the Republic of Armenia. 2. Subsoils and water resources shall be exclusive property of the State.

article_id: Article 100. Extraordinary Sessions and Sittings of the National Assembly
text_snippet: 1. An extraordinary session or sitting of the National Assembly shall be convened by the National Assembly Chairman by the initiative of at least one quarter of the total number of parliamentarians or

article_id: Article 101. Publicity of Sittings of the National Assembly
text_snippet: 1. Sittings of the National Assembly shall be public. 2. By proposal of at least one fifth of the total number of parliamentarians or of the Government, the National Assembly may take a decision, by m

article_id: Article 102. Quorum of National Assembly Sittings
text_snippet: A sitting of the National Assembly shall have quorum if more than half of the total number of parliamentarians have registered at the beginning of the sitting.

article_id: Article 103. The Adoption of Laws, National Assembly Decisions, Statements, and Addresses
text_snippet: 1. Laws and National Assembly decisions, statements, and addresses shall, except for cases stipulated by the Constitution, be adopted by majority vote of parliamentarians participating in the voting,

article_id: Article 104. The National Assembly Chairman and Deputy Chairmen, and the National Assembly Council
text_snippet: 1. The National Assembly shall elect from among its members the Chairman and three Deputy Chairmen of the National Assembly. One of the Deputy Chairmen is elected from among the parliamentarians inclu

article_id: Article 105. The Factions of the National Assembly
text_snippet: 1. The factions shall facilitate the formation of the political will of the National Assembly. 2. The factions shall include parliamentarians only of the same party or the same alliance of parties.

article_id: Article 106. The Standing Committees of the National Assembly
text_snippet: 1. For the purposes of preliminary discussion of draft laws and other issues pertaining to the authority of the National Assembly and of presenting opinions thereon to the National Assembly, as well a

article_id: Article 107. Temporary Committees of the National Assembly
text_snippet: For purposes of discussing certain draft laws, draft National Assembly decisions, draft statements, and draft addresses, as well as matters related to ethics of parliamentarians and of presenting opin

article_id: Article 108. Inquiry Committees of the National Assembly
text_snippet: 1. By demand of at least one quarter of the total number of parliamentarians, an inquiry committee of the National Assembly shall be formed by virtue of law for the purpose of establishing facts that

article_id: Article 109. Legislative Initiative
text_snippet: 1. A parliamentarian, a faction of the National Assembly, and the Government shall have the right of legislative initiative. 2. The author of the legislative initiative may at any time recall the draf

article_id: Article 11. The Economic Order
text_snippet: The basis of the economic order in the Republic of Armenia shall be the social market economy, which shall be based on private property, freedom of economic activity, free economic competition, and th

article_id: Article 110. Adoption of the State Budget
text_snippet: 1. The National Assembly shall adopt the state budget upon submission by the Government. The state budget shall include all of the state’s revenues and expenditures in the manner stipulated by law. 2.

article_id: Article 111. Oversight of State Budget Execution
text_snippet: 1. The National Assembly shall exercise oversight of state budget execution, as well as over the use of loans and debt received from foreign states and international organizations. 2. The National Ass

article_id: Article 112. Oral and Written Questions of Parliamentarians
text_snippet: 1. In one of the sittings convened during the sittings week of the regular session, the Government members shall answer the oral questions of parliamentarians. The National Assembly shall not adopt de

article_id: Article 113. Interpellations
text_snippet: 1. The factions of the National Assembly shall have the right to address the Government members with written interpellations. The Government members shall respond to the interpellation within no later

article_id: Article 114. Deliberations on Urgent Topics
text_snippet: In one of the sittings convened during the sittings week of the regular session, if so demanded by at least one quarter of the total number of parliamentarians, deliberations on urgent topics of publi

article_id: Article 115. Expressing Non-Confidence in the Prime Minister
text_snippet: 1. A draft decision of the National Assembly on expressing non-confidence in the Prime Minister may be presented by at least one third of the total number of parliamentarians, provided that the draft

article_id: Article 116. Ratification, Suspension, or Renunciation of International Treaties
text_snippet: 1. The National Assembly shall ratify, suspend, or renounce international treaties that: Concern the fundamental rights and freedoms, as well as obligations of the human being and citizen; Have a poli

article_id: Article 117. Amnesty
text_snippet: By proposal of the Government, the National Assembly may adopt a law on amnesty by majority vote of the total number of parliamentarians.

article_id: Article 118. Declaring War and Establishing Peace
text_snippet: 1. By proposal of the Government, the National Assembly may adopt a decision on declaring war or establishing peace by majority vote of the total number of parliamentarians. 2. If it is impossible to

article_id: Article 119. Martial Law
text_snippet: 1. In the event of an armed attack against the Republic of Armenia or imminent threat thereof or declaration of war, the Government shall declare a martial law and deliver an address to the people, an

article_id: Article 12. Preservation of the Environment and Sustainable Development
text_snippet: 1. The state shall promote the preservation, improvement, and regeneration of the environment, and the reasonable utilization of natural resources, governed by the principle of sustainable development

article_id: Article 120. State of Emergency
text_snippet: 1. In the event of an imminent threat to the constitutional order, the Government shall declare a state of emergency and take measures appropriate in the situation and address the people thereon. 2. I

article_id: Article 121. Administrative-Territorial Units and Division
text_snippet: Marzes and communities shall be the administrative-territorial units of the Republic of Armenia. The administrative-territorial division shall be defined by law upon submission by the Government.

article_id: Article 122. Autonomous Bodies
text_snippet: 1. To safeguard the exercise of fundamental rights and freedoms of the human being and citizen, as well as to protect fundamental public interests enshrined in the Constitution, autonomous bodies may

article_id: Article 123. Status and Functions of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: 1. The President of the Republic shall be the head of the state. 2. The President of the Republic shall observe compliance with the Constitution. 3. In exercising his powers, the President of the Repu

article_id: Article 124. Term of Office of and Requirements on the President of the Republic
text_snippet: 1. The President of the Republic shall be elected for a seven-year term. 2. Everyone who has attained the age of 40, has been a citizen of only the Republic of Armenia for the preceding six years, has

article_id: Article 125. Election Procedure of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: 1. The President of the Republic shall be elected by the National Assembly. 2. The regular election of the President of the Republic shall be held no earlier than 40 days and no later than 30 days bef

article_id: Article 126. Extraordinary Election of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: In the event of the impeachment of the President of the Republic, impossibility to discharge his powers, or his resignation or death, an extraordinary election of the President of the Republic shall b

article_id: Article 127. The President of the Republic Assuming Office
text_snippet: 1. The President of the Republic shall assume office on the day on which the powers of the previous President of the Republic end. 2. A President of the Republic elected through an extraordinary elect

article_id: Article 128. Address by the President of the Republic
text_snippet: The President of the Republic may deliver an address to the National Assembly on matters pertaining to his authority.

article_id: Article 129. The Signing and Publication of a Law
text_snippet: 1. The President of the Republic of Armenia shall sign and publish a law adopted by the National Assembly within a 21-day period or, within the same period, apply to the Constitutional Court with the

article_id: Article 13. Foreign Policy
text_snippet: The foreign policy of the Republic of Armenia shall be conducted on the basis of international law with the aim of establishing good-neighborly and mutually-beneficial relations with all states.

article_id: Article 130. Accepting the Resignation of the Government
text_snippet: In the cases stipulated by Article 158 of the Constitution, the President of the Republic shall immediately accept the resignation of the Government.

article_id: Article 131. Changes in the Composition of the Government
text_snippet: The President of the Republic shall make changes in the composition of the Government by proposal of the Prime Minister.

article_id: Article 132. Powers of the President of the Republic in the Area of Foreign Policy
text_snippet: 1. The President of the Republic shall, in the cases and manner stipulated by law: Conclude international treaties by proposal of the Government; Appoint and recall diplomatic representatives in forei

article_id: Article 133. Powers of the President of the Republic in the Area of Armed Forces
text_snippet: 1. By proposal of the Prime Minister, the President of the Republic shall, in the cases and manner stipulated by law, appoint and dismiss the supreme command of the armed forces and other troops. 2. B

article_id: Article 134. Resolution of Issues Related to Citizenship
text_snippet: In the cases and manner stipulated by law, the President of the Republic shall resolve issues related to the granting and termination of citizenship of the Republic of Armenia.

article_id: Article 135. Granting Pardon
text_snippet: In the cases and manner stipulated by law, the President of the Republic shall resolve the issue of granting pardon to convicted persons.

article_id: Article 136. Decorating with Awards and Granting Honorary Titles
text_snippet: In the cases and manner stipulated by law, the President of the Republic shall decorate with orders and medals of the Republic of Armenia and grant honorary titles.

article_id: Article 137. Awarding the Highest Ranks
text_snippet: In the cases and manner stipulated by law, the President of the Republic shall award the highest ranks.

article_id: Article 138. Temporary Appointment of Officials
text_snippet: If the National Assembly fails, within a three-month period, to elect the respective officials in the manner stipulated by Paragraph 3 of Article 174, Paragraph 1 of Article 177, Paragraph 1 of Articl

article_id: Article 139. Orders and Decrees of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: 1. In exercising his powers, the President of the Republic shall issue orders and decrees. 2. In the cases stipulated by Articles 131-137, Paragraph 3 of Article 155, and Paragraphs 3, 4, 6, and 7 of

article_id: Article 14. The Armed Forces and Defense
text_snippet: 1. The armed forces of the Republic of Armenia shall ensure the defense, security, and territorial integrity of the Republic of Armenia, and the inviolability of its borders. 2. The armed forces of th

article_id: Article 140. Immunity of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: 1. The President of the Republic shall be immune. 2. During and after the term of his office, the President of the Republic may not be prosecuted and held liable for actions stemming from his status.

article_id: Article 141. Impeachment of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: 1. The President of the Republic may be impeached for state treason, another grave crime, or for gravely breaching the Constitution. 2. To obtain a conclusion on the existence of grounds for impeachin

article_id: Article 142. Resignation of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: The President of Republic shall present his resignation to the National Assembly. The resignation shall be deemed accepted from the moment of promulgating it in the manner stipulated by law.

article_id: Article 143. Impossibility for the President of the Republic to Discharge His Powers
text_snippet: In case of grave illness of the President of Republic or the existence of other insurmountable obstacles to the discharge of his powers, which enduringly render the discharge of such powers impossible

article_id: Article 144. Temporary Discharge of Powers of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: In case of impeachment of the President of the Republic, the impossibility for the President to discharge his powers, or the resignation or death of the President, and before the newly-elected Preside

article_id: Article 145. Support to the Activities of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: 1. The procedure of formation of the staff of the President of the Republic shall be stipulated by law. In the cases and manner stipulated by law, the President of the Republic shall make appointments

article_id: Article 146. Status and Functions of the Government
text_snippet: 1. The Government shall be the highest body of the executive power. 2. Based on its program, the Government shall develop and implement the domestic and foreign policies of the state. 3. The Governmen

article_id: Article 147. Composition and Structure of the Government
text_snippet: 1. The Government shall consist of the Prime Minister, Deputy Prime Ministers, and ministers. 2. The list of ministries and the procedure of activities of the Government shall be stipulated by law upo

article_id: Article 148. Requirements on Government Members
text_snippet: 1. A Government member shall meet the requirements presented to a parliamentarian. 2. Government members shall be subject to the incompatibility requirements stipulated for a parliamentarian. Addition

article_id: Article 149. Election and Appointment of the Prime Minister
text_snippet: 1. Immediately after the commencement of the term of office of the newly-elected National Assembly, the President of the Republic shall appoint as Prime Minister the candidate nominated by the parliam

article_id: Article 15. Promotion of Culture, Education, and Science, Protection of the Armenian language and Cultural Heritage
text_snippet: 1. The state shall promote the development of culture, education, and science. 2. The Armenian language and the cultural heritage shall be under the care and protection of the state.

article_id: Article 150. Formation of the Government
text_snippet: The Government shall be formed within a 15-day period of the appointment of the Prime Minister. After his appointment, the Prime Minister shall within a five-day period propose to the President of the

article_id: Article 151. The Program of the Government
text_snippet: 1. Within a 20-day period of the formation of the Government, the Prime Minister shall present to the National Assembly the Program of the Government. 2. The National Assembly shall approve the Progra

article_id: Article 152. Powers of the Prime Minister and Other Members of the Government
text_snippet: 1. The Prime Minister shall, within the framework of the Program of the Government, determine the general guidelines of the Government’s policy, direct the activities of the Government, and coordinate

article_id: Article 153. Sittings and Decisions of the Government
text_snippet: 1. The Prime Minister shall invite and chair the sittings of the Government. 2. Decisions of the Government shall be signed by the Prime Minister. 3. The Government shall have the power to adopt sub-l

article_id: Article 154. Economic and Financial Policies
text_snippet: 1. The Government shall implement coherent state finance-economic, credit, and tax policies. 2. The Government shall administer the state property.

article_id: Article 155. The Armed Forces
text_snippet: 1. The armed forces shall be subordinate to the Government. A decision on engagement of the armed forces shall be taken by the Government. In case of urgent necessity, a decision on engagement of the

article_id: Article 156. Annual Report of the Government to the National Assembly
text_snippet: For each year, the Government shall present a report to the National Assembly on the implementation progress and results of its Program.

article_id: Article 157. The Question of Confidence in the Government
text_snippet: 1. The Government may put forward the question on confidence in the Government with respect to the adoption of a draft law presented by the Government. The draft decision on expressing confidence in t

article_id: Article 158. Resignation of the Government
text_snippet: The Government shall present its resignation to the President of the Republic on the day of the first session of the newly-elected National Assembly, on the day of expressing non-confidence in the Gov

article_id: Article 159. Bodies of the State Administration System
text_snippet: The bodies of the state administration system shall be the ministries, as well as other bodies subordinate to the Government, the Prime Minister, and the ministries, the powers and procedure of format

text_snippet: Family, being the natural and fundamental cell of society and the basis for the preservation and reproduction of the population, as well as motherhood and childhood shall be under special protection a

article_id: Article 160. Implementation of the Regional Policy of the Government
text_snippet: 1. The Government shall implement its regional policy in the marzes through the marz governors. 2. The marz governors shall be appointed and dismissed by the Government. The marz governors shall coord

article_id: Article 161. The Public Council
text_snippet: The Public Council shall be a body consultative to the Government. The procedure of the formation and operation of the Public Council shall be stipulated by law. CHAPTER 7. COURTS AND THE SUPREME JUDI

article_id: Article 162. The Administration of Justice
text_snippet: 1. In the Republic of Armenia, justice shall be administered solely by courts in accordance with the Constitution and laws. 2. Any interference with the administration of justice shall be prohibited.

article_id: Article 163. The Courts
text_snippet: 1. The Constitutional Court, the Cassation Court, appellate courts, general jurisdiction first instance courts, and the administrative court shall operate in the Republic of Armenia. In cases prescrib

article_id: Article 164. The Status of a Judge
text_snippet: 1. When administering justice, a judge shall be independent and impartial and act only in accordance with the Constitution and the laws. 2. A judge may not be held liable for opinions expressed or jud

article_id: Article 165. Requirements on Judge Candidates
text_snippet: 1. A lawyer with higher education, who has attained the age of 40, is a citizen of only the Republic of Armenia, has voting right, and has strong professional qualities and at least 15 years of profes

article_id: Article 166. The Judge Election and Appointment Procedure
text_snippet: 1. Judges of the Constitutional Court shall be elected by the National Assembly by at least three fifths of the votes of the total number of parliamentarians, for a 12-year term. The Constitutional Co

article_id: Article 167. The Constitutional Court
text_snippet: 1. Constitutional justice shall be administered by the Constitutional Court, ensuring the primacy of the Constitution. 2. When administering justice, the Constitutional Court shall be independent and

article_id: Article 168. Powers of the Constitutional Court

article_id: Article 17. The State and Religious Organizations
text_snippet: 1. The freedom of activity of religious organizations shall be guaranteed in the Republic of Armenia. 2. Religious organizations shall be separate from the state.

article_id: Article 170. Decisions and Opinions of the Constitutional Court
text_snippet: 1. The Constitutional Court shall adopt decisions and opinions. 2. Decisions and opinions of the Constitutional Court shall be final and shall enter into force at the moment of being published. 3. The

article_id: Article 171. The Cassation Court
text_snippet: 1. With the exception of the domain of constitutional justice, the Cassation Court shall be the highest judicial instance in the Republic of Armenia. 2. By reviewing judicial acts within the scope of

article_id: Article 172. The Appellate Courts
text_snippet: Appellate Courts are the judicial instance that reviews the judicial acts of first instance courts within the framework of powers stipulated by law.

article_id: Article 173. The Supreme Judicial Council
text_snippet: The Supreme Judicial Council is an independent state body that shall safeguard the independence of courts and judges.

article_id: Article 174. Composition and Formation Procedure of the Supreme Judicial Council
text_snippet: 1. The Supreme Judicial Council shall consist of 10 members. 2. Five of the members of the Supreme Judicial Council shall be elected by the General Assembly of Judges from among judges having at least

article_id: Article 175. Powers of the Supreme Judicial Council

article_id: Article 176. The Prosecution Office
text_snippet: 1. The prosecution office is a unified system that is led by the Prosecutor General. 2. The prosecution office shall, in the cases and manner stipulated by law: Initiate criminal prosecution; Supervis

article_id: Article 177. The Prosecutor General

article_id: Article 178. The Investigative Organs
text_snippet: 1. The investigative organs shall, in the cases and manner stipulated by law, organize and conduct the pre-trial criminal proceedings. 2. The status and powers of investigative organs and the procedur

article_id: Article 180. Communities
text_snippet: 1. A community is the whole of the population of one or several settlements. 2. A community shall be a public law legal entity.

article_id: Article 181. Elections of Local Self-Government Bodies

article_id: Article 182. Community Issues and the Powers of Local Self-Government Bodies

article_id: Article 183. Direct Participation in the Administration of Community Affairs

article_id: Article 184. Community Property
text_snippet: 1. A community shall have property right on land and other property. 2. Land located within the territory of a community, with the exception of land owned by the state, as well as by natural persons o

article_id: Article 185. Community Budget, Local Taxes, Duties, and Fees
text_snippet: 1. A community shall have its budget, which shall be adopted by the community council upon presentation by the community mayor. 2. The procedure of community budget revenue formation and expenditure e

article_id: Article 186. The Financing of Communities

article_id: Article 187. Local Self-Government in Yerevan
text_snippet: Yerevan is a community. The peculiarities of local self-government in Yerevan shall be stipulated by law.

article_id: Article 188. Legal and Professional Oversight
text_snippet: 1. The Government’s authorized body shall, in the cases and manner stipulated by law, perform legal oversight of the exercise of the community’s own powers. 2. The Government’s authorized bodies shall

article_id: Article 189. Inter-Community Unions

text_snippet: 1. The Republic of Armenia shall carry out a policy aimed at developing comprehensive ties and preserving Armenianness with the Armenian Diaspora, and shall facilitate repatriation. 2. Based on intern

article_id: Article 190. Merger and Separation of Communities
text_snippet: In view of public interests, communities may be merged or separated by law. When adopting the respective law, the National Assembly shall be obliged to listen to the opinion of such communities. CHAPT

article_id: Article 20. The State Language of the Republic of Armenia
text_snippet: The Armenian language shall be the state language of the Republic of Armenia.

article_id: Article 200. Main Goals and Functions of the Central Bank
text_snippet: 1. The national bank of the Republic of Armenia is the Central Bank. The Central Bank shall be independent in the performance of functions reserved for it by the Constitution and by law. 2. The main g

article_id: Article 203. Unamendable Articles of the Constitution
text_snippet: Articles 1, 2, 3, and 203 of the Constitution shall not be amended.

article_id: Article 22. The Capital of the Republic of Armenia
text_snippet: The capital of the Republic of Armenia is Yerevan. CHAPTER 2. FUNDAMENTAL RIGHTS AND FREEDOMS OF THE HUMAN BEING AND THE CITIZEN

article_id: Article 220. Tenure of the Members of Bodies Prescribed by Chapters 12-14 of the Constitution
text_snippet: After the entry into force of Chapters 12-14 of the Constitution, the members of the bodies prescribed by those Chapters shall continue to serve until the end of the term of their office stipulated by

article_id: Article 23. Human Dignity
text_snippet: Human dignity is inviolable.

article_id: Article 24. The Right to Life
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to life. 2. No one may be arbitrarily deprived of life. 3. No one shall be condemned to the death penalty, or executed.

article_id: Article 25. The Right to Physical and Mental Integrity
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to physical and mental integrity. 2. The right to physical and mental integrity may be restricted only by law, with the aim of protecting state security, preventing or

article_id: Article 26. The Prohibition of Torture, Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment
text_snippet: 1. No one shall be subjected to torture, inhuman or degrading treatment or punishment. 2. Corporal punishments shall be prohibited. 3. Persons deprived of liberty shall have the right to humane treatm

article_id: Article 27. Personal Liberty
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to personal liberty. No one may be deprived of personal liberty except in the following cases and in the manner stipulated by law: A competent court has convicted the

article_id: Article 28. General Equality before the Law
text_snippet: Everyone shall be equal before the law.

article_id: Article 29. The Prohibition of Discrimination
text_snippet: Any discrimination based on sex, race, skin color, ethnic or social origin, genetic features, language, religion, worldview, political or any other views, belonging to a national minority, property st

article_id: Article 3. The Human Being, His Dignity, Fundamental Rights, and Freedoms
text_snippet: 1. The human being shall be the supreme value in the Republic of Armenia. The inalienable dignity of the human being shall be the integral basis of his rights and freedoms. 2. The respect for and prot

article_id: Article 30. Equality of Rights between Women and Men
text_snippet: Women and men shall have equal rights.

article_id: Article 31. Inviolability of Private and Family Life and of Honor and Reputation
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to inviolability of his private and family life, honor and reputation. 2. The right to inviolability of private and family life may be restricted only by law with the

article_id: Article 32. Inviolability of the Home
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to inviolability of the home. 2. The inviolability of the home may be restricted only by law with the aim of protecting state security, the economic well-being of the

article_id: Article 33. Freedom and Confidentiality of Communication
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to freedom and confidentiality of correspondence, telephone conversations and other means of communication. 2. The freedom and confidentiality of communication may be

article_id: Article 34. The Protection of Personal Data
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to protection of data concerning him. 2. Personal data shall be processed in good faith for purposes stipulated by law, with the consent of the person or without such

article_id: Article 35. Freedom to Marry
text_snippet: 1. A man and a woman of marriageable age shall have the right to marry each other and form a family by free expression of their will. The age of marriage and the procedure of marrying and divorcing sh

article_id: Article 36. Rights and Obligations of Parents
text_snippet: 1. Parents shall have the right and obligation to take care of the rearing, education, health, and comprehensive and harmonious development of their children. 2. Deprivation or limitation of parental

article_id: Article 37. The Rights of a Child
text_snippet: 1. A child shall have the right to express his opinion freely, which shall be taken into consideration in matters concerning the child in accordance with his age and maturity. 2. In matters concerning

article_id: Article 38. The Right to Education
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to education. The programs and duration of compulsory education shall be stipulated by law. Secondary education in state educational institutions is free of charge. 2.

article_id: Article 39. The Right of a Human Being to Act Freely
text_snippet: A human being shall be free to do all that does not violate the rights of others and does not contradict the Constitution and laws. No one may bear obligations that are not stipulated by law.

article_id: Article 4. The Principle of Separation and Balance of the Powers
text_snippet: State power shall be exercised in accordance with the Constitution and the laws, based on the separation and balance of the legislative, executive, and judicial powers.

article_id: Article 40. The Right to Freedom of Movement
text_snippet: 1. Everyone legally present in the territory of the Republic Armenia shall have the right to freedom of movement and the right to choose a place of residence. 2. Everyone shall have the right to exit

article_id: Article 41. The Freedom of Thought, Conscience, and Religion
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to the freedom of thought, conscience, and religion. This right shall include the freedom to change one’s religion or beliefs and the freedom, either alone or in commu

article_id: Article 42. The Freedom of Expression of Opinion
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to freely express his opinion. This right shall include freedom to hold own opinions, as well as to seek, receive, and impart information and ideas by any means of inf

article_id: Article 43. The Freedom of Creation
text_snippet: Everyone shall have the freedom of literary, fine arts, scientific, and technical creation.

article_id: Article 44. The Freedom of Assembly
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to freely organize and participate in peaceful and unarmed assemblies. 2. In cases stipulated by law, outdoor assemblies shall be conducted on the basis of prior notif

article_id: Article 45. The Freedom of Association
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to the freedom of association with others, including the right to form and to join trade unions for the protection of labor interests. No one shall be compelled to joi

article_id: Article 46. The Right to Create a Party and to Become a Member of a Party
text_snippet: 1. Every citizen shall have the right to create a party with other citizens and the right to become a member of a party. No one shall be compelled to become a member of a party. 2. Judges, prosecutors

article_id: Article 47. The Right to Citizenship of the Republic of Armenia
text_snippet: 1. A child born to citizens of the Republic of Armenia shall be a citizen of the Republic of Armenia. 2. Every child whose one parent is a citizen of the Republic of Armenia shall have the right to ac

article_id: Article 48. Right to Vote and Right to Participate in a Referendum
text_snippet: 1. Citizens of the Republic of Armenia, which have attained the age of 18 on the day of an election to the National Assembly or on the day of a referendum, shall have the right to vote in such electio

article_id: Article 49. The Right to Enter the Public Service
text_snippet: Every citizen shall have the right to enter the public service on an equal basis. Details shall be stipulated by law.

article_id: Article 5. The Hierarchy of Legal Norms
text_snippet: 1. The Constitution shall have supreme legal force. 2. Laws shall conform to the constitutional laws, and sub-legislative normative legal acts shall conform to the constitutional laws and laws. 3. In

article_id: Article 50. The Right to Proper Administration
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to the impartial and fair examination by administrative authorities, within a reasonable period, of cases concerning him. 2. During the administrative procedure, every

article_id: Article 51. The Right to Receive Information
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to receive information on the activities of state and local self-government bodies and officials and to become acquainted with documents about such activities. 2. The

article_id: Article 52. The Right to Apply to the Human Rights Defender
text_snippet: Everyone shall have the right to receive the support of the Human Rights Defender, in case of violation of his rights and freedoms enshrined in the Constitution and laws by state and local self-govern

article_id: Article 53. The Right to Submit a Petition
text_snippet: Everyone shall have the right to submit, individually or in community with others, petitions to state and local self-government bodies and officials and to receive an appropriate reply within a reason

article_id: Article 54. The Right to Political Asylum
text_snippet: Everyone subject to political persecution shall have the right to seek political asylum in the Republic of Armenia. The procedure and conditions of granting political asylum shall be stipulated by law

article_id: Article 55. The Prohibition of Deportation or Extradition
text_snippet: 1. No one may be deported or extradited to a foreign state, if there is a real danger that such person may be subjected to the death penalty, torture, inhuman or degrading treatment or punishment in t

article_id: Article 56. The Right to Preserve National and Ethnic Identity
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to preserve his national and ethnic identity. 2. Persons belonging to national minorities shall have the right to preserve and develop their traditions, religion, lang

article_id: Article 57. The Freedom to Choose Employment and the Labor Rights
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to free choice of employment. 2. Every worker shall have the right to protection in case of groundless dismissal from employment. The grounds of dismissal from employm

article_id: Article 58. The Right to a Strike
text_snippet: 1. Workers shall have the right to strike for the protection of their economic, social, or labor interests. The procedure of conducting a strike shall be stipulated by law. 2. The right to a strike ma

article_id: Article 59. The Freedom of Economic Activities and Guaranteeing Economic Competition
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to engage in economic, including entrepreneurial activities. The conditions and procedure of exercising this right shall be stipulated by law. 2. The restrictions of c

article_id: Article 6. The Principle of Legality
text_snippet: 1. State and local self-government bodies and officials shall have the power to perform only such acts for which they are empowered by the Constitution or laws. 2. Bodies prescribed by the Constitutio

article_id: Article 60. The Right to Property
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to own, use, and dispose at his discretion the lawfully-acquired property. 2. The right of inheritance shall be guaranteed. 3. The right to property may be restricted

article_id: Article 61. The Right to Judicial Protection and the Right to Apply to International Bodies of Human Rights Protection
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to effective judicial protection of his rights and freedoms. 2. Everyone shall, for the protection of his rights and freedoms, and in conformity with the international

article_id: Article 62. Right to Compensation for Damage
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to compensation for damage inflicted by the unlawful actions or inaction of state and local self-government bodies and officials, and in cases stipulated by law, also

article_id: Article 63. The Right to a Fair Trial
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to a fair and public hearing of his case within a reasonable period by an independent and impartial court. 2. In the cases and manner stipulated by law, the court proc

article_id: Article 64. The Right to Receive Legal Aid
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall have the right to receive legal aid. In cases stipulated by law, legal aid shall be provided at the expense of state funds. 2. With the aim of ensuring legal aid, the activities of a

article_id: Article 65. The Right to Be Exempted from the Duty to Testify
text_snippet: No one shall be obliged to testify about himself, his spouse, or his close relatives, if it can be reasonably presumed that it may subsequently be used against him or them. The law may stipulate other

article_id: Article 66. The Presumption of Innocence
text_snippet: A person accused of a crime shall be presumed innocent until his guilt is proven in the manner stipulated by law by a court judgment that has entered into legal force.

article_id: Article 67. The Right to Defend Oneself from Charges
text_snippet: Everyone charged with a crime shall have: 1. The right to be informed promptly, in a language which he understands and in detail, of the nature and cause of the charge. 2. The right to defend himself

article_id: Article 68. The Prohibition of Being Tried Twice
text_snippet: 1. No one may be tried twice for the same act. 2. The provisions of Paragraph 1 of this Article shall not prevent the review of a case in accordance with law when new or newly-discovered circumstances

article_id: Article 69. The Right of a Convicted Person to Appeal
text_snippet: Everyone convicted for committing a crime shall have the right to have the court judgment rendered in respect of him to be reviewed by a higher judicial instance based on the grounds and in the manner

article_id: Article 7. The Suffrage Principles
text_snippet: Elections of the National Assembly and community councils, as well as referenda shall be carried out on the basis of universal, equal, free, and direct suffrage, by secret vote.

article_id: Article 70. The Right to Request a Pardon
text_snippet: Every convict shall have the right to request a pardon, including the right to request mitigation of the imposed sentence. Details shall be stipulated by law.

article_id: Article 71. The Principle of Guilt and the Principle of Proportionality of Punishment
text_snippet: 1. The basis for punishing a person who committed a crime shall be his guilt. 2. The penalty stipulated by law, as well as the imposed penalty type and magnitude shall be proportionate to the committe

article_id: Article 72. The Principle of Legality in Defining Crimes and Imposing Penalties
text_snippet: No one shall be convicted for an action or inaction that was not a crime at the time of its commission. A penalty that is more severe than the one applicable at the time of committing the crime may no

article_id: Article 73. The Retrospective Effect of Laws and Other Legal Acts
text_snippet: 1. Laws and other legal acts that cause a person’s legal situation to deteriorate shall not have retrospective effect. 2. Laws and other legal acts improving a person’s legal situation shall have retr

article_id: Article 74. The Applicability of Fundamental Rights and Freedoms in Respect of Legal Persons
text_snippet: The fundamental rights and freedoms shall extend also to legal persons to the extent such rights and freedoms are by their essence applicable to them.

article_id: Article 75. Organizational Structures and Procedures for the Exercise of Fundamental Rights and Freedoms
text_snippet: When regulating fundamental rights and freedoms, laws shall define the organizational structures and procedures necessary for their effective exercise.

article_id: Article 76. Restrictions of Fundamental Rights and Freedoms in Emergency Situations or during Martial Law
text_snippet: In a state of emergency or during martial law, fundamental rights and freedoms of the human being and the citizen, with the exception of those stipulated by Articles 23–26, 28–30, 35–37, Paragraph 1 o

article_id: Article 77. The Prohibition of Abuse of Fundamental Rights and Freedoms
text_snippet: It shall be prohibited to use fundamental rights and freedoms for the purpose of violently overthrowing the constitutional order or inciting national, racial, or religious hatred, or preaching violenc

article_id: Article 78. The Principle of Proportionality
text_snippet: The means chosen for restricting fundamental rights and freedoms shall be suitable and necessary for the achievement of the aim stipulated by the Constitution. The means chosen for the restriction sha

article_id: Article 79. The Principle of Certainty
text_snippet: When restricting fundamental rights and freedoms, the laws shall define the grounds and scope of such restrictions and be sufficiently certain for the holders and addressees of such rights and freedom

article_id: Article 8. Ideological Pluralism and the Multipartisan System
text_snippet: 1. Ideological pluralism and the multipartisan system shall be guaranteed in the Republic of Armenia. 2. Parties shall be formed and operate freely. Equal legal opportunities for activities of the par

article_id: Article 80. Inviolability of the Essence of Provisions on Fundamental Rights and Freedoms
text_snippet: The essence of provisions on fundamental rights and freedoms enshrined in this Chapter shall be inviolable.

article_id: Article 81. Fundamental Rights and Freedoms and the International Legal Practice
text_snippet: 1. The practice of bodies operating on the basis of international human rights treaties, which have been ratified by the Republic of Armenia, shall be taken into account when interpreting the provisio

article_id: Article 82. Working Conditions
text_snippet: Every worker shall, in accordance with law, have the right to healthy, safe, and dignifying working conditions, to limitation of the maximum working time, to daily and weekly rest, and to annual paid

article_id: Article 83. Social Security
text_snippet: Everyone shall, in accordance with law, have the right to social security in cases of maternity, having many children, illness, disability, workplace accidents, need of care, loss of breadwinner, old

article_id: Article 84. Dignifying Existence and Minimum Salary
text_snippet: 1. Every person in need and every elderly person shall, in accordance with law, have the right to a dignifying existence. 2. The amount of minimum salary shall be stipulated by law.

article_id: Article 85. Health Care
text_snippet: 1. Everyone shall, in accordance with law, have the right to protection of health. 2. The law shall stipulate the list of basic medical services provided free of charge and the procedure of their prov

article_id: Article 86. The Main Objectives of State Policy
text_snippet: The main objectives of state policy in the economic, social, and cultural spheres shall be: 1. To improve the business environment and to promote entrepreneurship; 2. To support population employment

article_id: Article 87. Fulfillment of the Main Objectives of State Policy
text_snippet: 1. To the extent of their powers and possibilities, state government and local self-government bodies shall be obliged to fulfill the objectives enshrined in Article 86 of the Constitution. 2. As part

article_id: Article 88. The Status and Functions of the National Assembly
text_snippet: 1. The National Assembly is the people’s representative body. 2. The National Assembly shall exercise the legislative power. 3. The National Assembly shall exercise oversight of the executive power, s

article_id: Article 89. The National Assembly Composition and Election Procedure
text_snippet: 1. The National Assembly shall consist of at least 101 parliamentarians. 2. In the manner stipulated by the Electoral Code, places shall be assigned in the National Assembly for representatives of nat

article_id: Article 9. Guaranteeing Local Self-Government
text_snippet: Local self-government is guaranteed in the Republic of Armenia as one of the essential foundations of democracy.

article_id: Article 90. Term of Office of the National Assembly
text_snippet: 1. The National Assembly shall be elected for a five-year term. 2. In case of a regular election, the term of office of the newly-elected National Assembly shall start at the moment of opening the fir

article_id: Article 91. Regular Election of the National Assembly
text_snippet: 1. A regular election of the National Assembly shall be held no earlier than 60 and no later than 50 days before the end of the term of office of the National Assembly. 2. During martial law or a stat

article_id: Article 92. Extraordinary Election of the National Assembly
text_snippet: 1. An extraordinary election of the National Assembly shall be held after dissolution of the National Assembly in the cases stipulated by Paragraph 3 of Article 149 or Paragraphs 3 and 4 of Article 15

article_id: Article 93. Setting Elections of the National Assembly
text_snippet: Regular and extraordinary elections of the National Assembly shall be set by the President of the Republic.

article_id: Article 94. Representation Mandate
text_snippet: A parliamentarian shall represent the whole people, shall not be bound by imperative mandate, and shall be guided by his conscience and beliefs.

article_id: Article 95. Incompatibility of the Parliamentarian Mandate
text_snippet: A parliamentarian may not hold office not stemming from his function in other state or local self-government bodies, or any office in commercial organizations, or engage in entrepreneurial activities

article_id: Article 96. The Immunity of a Parliamentarian
text_snippet: 1. During and after the term of his powers, a parliamentarian may not be prosecuted and held liable for the voting or opinions expressed in the framework of parliamentarian activities. 2. Criminal pro

article_id: Article 97. Remuneration Amount and Other Safeguards of Activities of a Parliamentarian
text_snippet: The remuneration amount and other safeguards of activities of a parliamentarian shall be stipulated by law.

article_id: Article 98. Cessation and Termination of Powers of a Parliamentarian
text_snippet: 1. The powers of a parliamentarian shall cease upon the expiration of the term of office of the National Assembly, loss of citizenship of the Republic of Armenia or acquisition of the citizenship of a

article_id: Article 99. Regular Sessions of the National Assembly
text_snippet: Regular sessions of the National Assembly shall be convened twice a year—from the third Monday of January to the third Thursday of June, and from the second Monday of September to the third Thursday o
